g.text: printk Log Levels and dmesg / Console Routing
Level extracted from first byte of format string
Modern pr_* Wrappers (preferred over raw printk)
Red / Orange → high-severity levels (0-3): always reach console
Green → info / notice (5-6): ring buffer only by default
Blue / Grey → debug (7): compiled out unless DEBUG defined
Dashed arrow → implicit / default path (no explicit level)
Use: kernel is about to die
Use: hardware failure, data loss imminent
Use: pr_debug() — compiled out without DEBUG
Kernel uses KERN_DEFAULT → resolves to KERN_WARNING (4)
Common mistake: printk("msg") instead of printk(KERN_INFO "msg")
Fix: always use pr_info() / pr_err() wrappers — prefix is automatic
log_buf | char* | pointer to circular buffer
log_first_seq | uint64_t | sequence of oldest record
log_next_seq | uint64_t | sequence of next record slot
log_first_idx | uint32_t | byte offset of oldest record
log_next_idx | uint32_t | byte offset for next write
Records: struct printk_log { u64 ts_nsec; u16 len; u8 level; ... }
Circular — oldest records overwritten when full
In RAM only — lost on power cycle / reboot
Dumps ALL records regardless of level
dmesg | grep mymod → filter by module name
Rule: level < console_loglevel → printed to console
Place at top of .c file before #include <linux/printk.h>
Avoids repeating module name in every format string
